setupWizard.autoDeployDesc: Deploy the agent immediately after creation
setupWizard.reviewDesc: Review your configuration before creating the agent

editor.formPlaceholder: Visual editor coming soon. Use the JSON tab to edit.
editor.cascadeMode: Changes will cascade to parent workflow and agent
editor.updateUsageHint: Update the selected workflows and agents to use the new version?
editor.invalidJson: Invalid JSON — switch to the JSON tab to fix.

apiCallsEditor.descriptionPlaceholder: Natural language description for LLM agents

llmEditor.enableHttpCallToolsDesc: Automatically expose all httpcall extensions from the workflow as LLM tools
llmEditor.httpCallToolsHint: Explicit httpcall URIs (in addition to auto-discovered ones)
llmEditor.mcpServersDesc: External MCP servers whose tools become available to the LLM
llmEditor.preRequestDesc: Set or transform properties before the LLM call executes.
llmEditor.postResponseDesc: Process LLM output: set properties, build output messages, and generate quick replies.
llmEditor.enableMcpCallToolsDesc: Automatically expose all mcpcalls extensions from the workflow as LLM tools
llmEditor.a2aAgentsDesc: Remote A2A-compatible agents whose skills become LLM tools
llmEditor.cascadeDesc: Try a cheap/fast model first. If confidence is too low, automatically escalate to a more powerful (and expensive) model. Saves costs without sacrificing quality.
llmEditor.cascadeStrategyHint: Sequential tries cheap first, escalates on low confidence
llmEditor.cascadeEvalHint: How to determine if a response is good enough
llmEditor.cascadeInAgent: Also use cascade in Agent Mode (with tools)
llmEditor.cascadeStepsDesc: Order matters: first step tried first. Last step is always accepted (set confidence to empty).
llmEditor.parallelExecutionDesc: Run independent tool calls concurrently instead of sequentially
llmEditor.toolRateLimitsDesc: Override the default rate for specific tools (calls/min)
llmEditor.retryDesc: Configure automatic retries for failed LLM API calls with exponential backoff.
llmEditor.ragDesc: Augment LLM responses with relevant documents retrieved from an embedding store.

coordinator.title: Coordinator Dashboard
coordinator.subtitle: Monitor conversation processing and manage dead-letter entries
coordinator.emptyHint: The coordinator service may still be starting up. Data will appear automatically.

contentEditor.fullscreenDescription: Full-screen content editor. Press Escape to close.

logs.title: Logs
logs.description: Monitor real-time log stream or search historical logs.
logs.noHistoryHint: Try adjusting the filters or search for a different agent or conversation.
logs.waitingHint: Logs will appear here as they stream in from the backend.

secrets.title: Secrets Vault
secrets.description: Manage encrypted secrets shared across all agents in a tenant. Values are never exposed.
secrets.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. OpenAI API key for production
secrets.storeWarning: The value will be encrypted with AES-256-GCM. It cannot be retrieved once stored — only replaced.
secrets.confirmDeleteMessage: Are you sure you want to permanently delete "{{key}}"? Any agent configs using this secret will fail to resolve. This cannot be undone.
secrets.emptyHint: No secrets stored for tenant "{{tenant}}" yet. Click "Add Secret" to create one.
secrets.scopeExplanation: Secrets are scoped per tenant and shared across all agents. Reference them in configs with ${eddivault:keyName}. Access is controlled by configuration authorship — only admins who write the config decide which secrets to use.
secrets.creatingFor: Storing in tenant "{{tenant}}" — available to all agents

audit.emptyHint: Enter a conversation or agent ID above to browse the audit trail.
audit.noResultsHint: Try a different ID or check that the conversation has been processed.

ragEditor.kbNameHint: Used to reference this KB from LLM tasks
ragEditor.embeddingParamsHint: Model name, API key (use ${vault:...} for secrets), base URL, etc.
ragEditor.overlapHint: Overlap helps maintain context between chunks
ragEditor.chunkBlocks: {{count}} chunks of ~{{size}} chars with {{overlap}} char overlap
ragEditor.retrievalHint: These defaults can be overridden per-task in the LLM configuration.
ragEditor.dropHint: Text, Markdown, or any text-based file
ragEditor.saveFirstIngestion: Save this knowledge base first to enable document ingestion.

welcome.title: Welcome to EDDI
welcome.subtitle: Your AI Agent Command Center
welcome.introText: EDDI Manager is your command center for building, testing, and operating conversational AI agents.
welcome.capabilitiesSubtitle: Everything you need to build production-ready AI agents
welcome.buildAgentsDesc: Create and configure AI agents with the wizard or from scratch
welcome.designWorkflowsDesc: Drag-and-drop pipeline builder with Rules, LLM chains, and tools
welcome.testRealtimeDesc: Chat with your agents with streaming responses and built-in debugging
welcome.monitorOperateDesc: Conversations, audit trails, secrets, and quotas — all in one place
welcome.readySubtitle: You can restart any tour from the sidebar Help menu

dashboard.title: Dashboard Tour
dashboard.step1Desc: The sidebar organizes everything into three zones: Management, Development, and Operations. You'll always know where to find things.
dashboard.step2Desc: Live counts of your agents, workflows, and conversations. Click any card to jump straight to that section.
dashboard.step3Desc: Jump straight to common tasks. The Agent Wizard is the fastest way to create your first agent — it takes under 2 minutes.
dashboard.step4Desc: Switch between light, dark, and system themes. EDDI Manager speaks 11 languages — pick yours from the language selector.

agents.title: Agents Tour
agents.step1Desc: The wizard walks you through creating a fully configured agent — choose a model, set a personality, enable tools, and deploy.
agents.step2Desc: For power users: create a blank agent and configure it with full JSON control. Perfect for advanced setups.
agents.step3Desc: Find agents instantly by name. Switch between card and list views with the toggle on the right.
agents.step4Desc: Each card shows the agent's name, version, and last modified time. Click any agent for its detail page with deploy, chat, and workflow controls.

workflows.title: Workflows Tour
workflows.step1Desc: Workflows define the processing pipeline — a sequence of tasks like LLM calls, Rules, and HTTP integrations.
workflows.step2Desc: Each workflow can be attached to one or more agents. Click to open the drag-and-drop pipeline editor.
workflows.step3Desc: Search by name and switch between card grid and sortable table views.

chat.title: Chat Tour
chat.step1Desc: Select a deployed agent to start a conversation. Only agents that have been deployed appear here.
chat.step2Desc: Toggle real-time token streaming. When enabled, the response appears word by word — great for debugging latency.
chat.step3Desc: Open the sidebar to browse and resume previous conversations.
chat.step4Desc: Type a message and press Enter. The agent responds using the LLM, tools, and workflows you've configured.

resources.title: Resources Tour
resources.step1Desc: Resources are the building blocks of workflows. Each type controls a different part of the agent's behavior.
resources.step2Desc: Define which AI model powers your agent, its system prompt, tools, MCP servers, and cascade settings. This is the brain.
resources.step3Desc: Rules control conversation flow. HTTP Calls connect to external APIs. Both can be used as LLM tools.

conversations.title: Conversations Tour
conversations.step1Desc: Find conversations quickly by ID, agent, or recent user messages.
conversations.step2Desc: Filter conversations by state: Active, In Progress, Ended, or Error.
conversations.step3Desc: Browse all sessions. Click any conversation card to open the timeline and debug.

groups.title: Discussion Groups Tour
groups.step1Desc: Create an orchestration group to let multiple agents collaborate and debate.
groups.step2Desc: Search through your existing multi-agent clusters.
groups.step3Desc: Manage your collaborative environments, edit their policies, or delete stale groups.

logs.title: System Logs Tour
logs.step1Desc: Switch between a real-time stream of system logs and historical log search.
logs.step2Desc: Filter the incoming live stream by Agent or by critical Log Level.
logs.step3Desc: Download the current filtered logs as a text file for external analysis.

secrets.title: Secrets Vault Tour
secrets.step1Desc: Securely store a new API key, token, or password that your agents can reference.
secrets.step2Desc: Filter the vault view to show secrets scoped to a specific tenant ID.
secrets.step3Desc: Learn how to dynamically inject secrets into your LLM configurations without hardcoding them.

audit.title: Audit Ledger Tour
audit.step1Desc: Search the immutable audit ledger by a specific Conversation ID or by an Agent version.
audit.step2Desc: Enter your ID and click search to pull the verifiable execution history.
audit.step3Desc: Export the retrieved audit records to JSON for compliance and reporting.

schedules.title: Schedules Tour
schedules.step1Desc: Set up a new cron job or continuous heartbeat to trigger agent workflows automatically.
schedules.step2Desc: Monitor the total, active, and failed schedules across your platform.
schedules.step3Desc: Manage your automated triggers natively: pause, resume, edit, or test-fire them.

quotas.title: Quotas Tour
quotas.step1Desc: Define hard limits on total agents, daily conversations, and token costs for this tenant.
quotas.step2Desc: Monitor how much of the allocated quota the tenant has consumed in real-time.
quotas.step3Desc: Save your adjusted limits. The platform will block traffic that breaches these ceilings.

coordinator.title: Coordinator Tour
coordinator.step1Desc: View the heartbeat and event source connected to the backend coordinator system.
coordinator.step2Desc: Monitor real-time task queues and active job execution across your cluster.
coordinator.step3Desc: Inspect failed tasks and systematically discard or retry them to maintain system stability.

orphans.title: Orphans Tour
orphans.step1Desc: Start a platform scan to detect loose configs disconnected from any agent.
orphans.step2Desc: Optionally toggle to include historically soft-deleted records in the purge.
orphans.step3Desc: Review the scanner's report, select stale objects, and permanently purge to reclaim DB space.

groups.emptyDescription: Groups let multiple agents collaborate on structured discussions.
groups.confirmDeleteDesc: This will permanently delete the group configuration.

memories.title: User Memory
memories.subtitle: Browse, search, and manage persistent user memories stored by agents
memories.enterUserId: Enter a User ID to browse their memories
memories.deleteAllDesc: This will permanently delete ALL memory entries for this user. This action cannot be undone.

properties.title: User Properties
properties.subtitle: Browse and manage longTerm slot-filling properties stored per user
properties.enterUserId: Enter a User ID to view their properties
properties.deleteAllDesc: This will permanently delete ALL properties for this user.

triggers.title: Agent Triggers
triggers.subtitle: Map intents to agent deployments for automatic routing
triggers.deleteDesc: Permanently delete the trigger for intent "{{intent}}"?
triggers.searchPlaceholder: Filter by intent or agent ID...

capabilities.title: Capability Discovery
capabilities.subtitle: Discover agents by their declared skills and capabilities
capabilities.noMatches: No agents found for this skill
